### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 17:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Recent from Google Drive

### Read Recent from Google Drive

Searches Google Drive for matching files/folders — the discovery step before download or processing.

Ports: ①→ Newest First · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Read Recent from Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Trim to Digest Size

### Trim to Digest Size

Caps the stream at 15 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Content (AI)

### Summarize Content (AI)

Map-reduce summarization chain — handles long content without blowing the context window.

Ports: ①→ Render as HTML · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Anthropic Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Summarize Content (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Anthropic API key

### Render as HTML

Converts markdown to HTML for rich rendering in email/CMS.

Next: Post Digest to Discord

### Post Digest to Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

Ports: ①→ Archive Digest in Google Sheets · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Archive Digest in Google Sheets

Appends a new row to 'Email Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Cap Batch Volume · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Archive Digest in Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Guard Non-Empty from

### Guard Non-Empty from

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Email Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Discord · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#inbox-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Deliver a weekly summary with Google Drive and Discord at scale

**Pattern:** Scheduled Digest · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Deliver a weekly summary with Google Drive and Discord at scale. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual inbox triage is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Read Recent from Google Drive → Newest First → Trim to Digest Size → Summarize Content (AI) → Render as HTML → Post Digest to Discord → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to scheduled digest without manual effort
- Anyone automating inbox triage in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Scales without extra headcount
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Anthropic API key; Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Summarization uses a Chain, not an Agent, because the step is fixed — there's no decision about which tool to call, just 'summarize this text.' Posting to multiple channels runs as parallel branches, not sequential sends, so a slow API on one channel never delays the message that was already ready on another.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a third notification channel and watch the Merge node's input count update.
2. Change 'Trim to Digest Size' from 15 to 5 and see how it narrows the summary's scope.
3. Swap the Summarization Chain for a Q&A Chain to answer questions instead of just recapping.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your inbox triage process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
